title: indigofera strobilifera subsp. lanuginosa
intro: Indigofera strobilifera subsp. lanuginosa, also known as hairy stick indigo, is a plant species in the legume family that is native to tropical and subtropical regions of Asia and Australia. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Indigofera strobilifera subsp. lanuginosa include reducing inflammation and fever, treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne, and potentially lowering blood sugar levels. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be made from its leaves, stems, and roots, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
